title: Smoky Light Syrian Vegan Bacon Soup
description: A fragrant, smoky lentil soup infused with vegan bacon, coconut milk and aromatic spices, served light and adventurous.
instructions: Preheat your sous‑vide machine to 63°C (145°F). Place the vegan bacon strips in a vacuum bag, sprinkle with ½ tsp smoked paprika, seal, and cook for 2 hours. Remove and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the tomato puree, lentils, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp ground cumin, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are just tender.
Reduce heat again, stir in the coconut milk, and season to taste. Simmer for another 5 minutes.
Slice the cooked vegan bacon into strips and add them to the pot. Let the soup simmer for an additional 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove from heat.
Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with chopped fresh parsley, and serve hot.
